title: The One with the Nap Partners

jokes.text: - I hope it's you. - Me too.
jokes.setup: Monica asks who should be her maid of honor, both friends express hope it's them
jokes.punchline: They both simultaneously hope for the same impossible outcome
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment of competitive friendship revealing itself through simultaneous identical hopes

jokes.text: Blah, blah, blah. Who is it?
jokes.setup: Monica gives a heartfelt preamble about loving them both
jokes.punchline: Phoebe cuts through the sentiment with blunt impatience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her directness cutting through social niceties with perfect comedic timing and character truth

jokes.text: - Yes! Oh. Hypothetically. Still.
jokes.setup: Monica explains hypothetical maid of honor rotation system
jokes.punchline: Phoebe gets excited then tries to dial it back while still being excited
jokes.explanation: Nice character beat showing Phoebe trying to contain her enthusiasm while failing to hide it

jokes.text: - Why is that the best part? - I don't have to.
jokes.setup: Monica claims letting them decide is 'the best part'
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals she just wants to avoid making the difficult choice herself
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of Monica's true motivation - avoiding confrontation by framing cowardice as generosity

jokes.text: Good luck! Oh, and good luck to you too!
jokes.setup: Stranger wishes Monica luck marrying Chandler
jokes.punchline: The sarcastic double-emphasis reveals this isn't a genuine well-wish
jokes.explanation: Sets up the mystery subplot with effective misdirection - seems nice but the repetition signals something's off

jokes.text: Well, we'll watch it a second time and it's Die Hard 2.
jokes.setup: Ross points out Joey rented Die Hard 1 again instead of Die Hard 2
jokes.punchline: Joey's absurd logic that watching the same movie twice makes it the sequel
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey logic - perfectly absurd reasoning delivered with complete confidence that only this character could sell

jokes.text: Dude, you didn't say Die Hard.
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey both say 'Die Hard!' enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Joey notices Chandler's lack of participation with hurt disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's need for group participation and his hurt when excluded

jokes.text: Well, John McClane had plans.
jokes.setup: Chandler says he has plans and needs to leave
jokes.punchline: Joey uses Die Hard protagonist as life advice/peer pressure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey moment - treating action movie characters as real-world role models with complete sincerity

jokes.text: ...and starts calling everybody 'bitch.' What are you talking about, bitch?
jokes.setup: Chandler warns about Joey getting worked up and calling people 'bitch'
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately proves the point by calling Chandler 'bitch'
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate payoff - Joey proves Chandler's point within seconds with zero self-awareness

jokes.text: What if I marry Ross? Or Joey? You wouldn't.
jokes.setup: Phoebe argues about future wedding possibilities
jokes.punchline: Rachel's immediate dismissal of Phoebe marrying their friends
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Rachel's assumptions about Phoebe's romantic prospects

jokes.text: I guess that was it.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks Rachel to let her finish her argument
jokes.punchline: Reveals she had nothing more to say
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan moment - Phoebe's dramatic request to finish followed by admission she had nothing more

jokes.text: Better than Chandler.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says she'll marry someone good
jokes.punchline: Her casual dig at Chandler as the bar to clear
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moment - her blunt assessment of Chandler as easily improvable upon

jokes.text: - Bye. - No touch. No touch.
jokes.setup: Chandler and Ross awkwardly part after their nap
jokes.punchline: They're so freaked out they can't even have normal physical contact
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing masculine discomfort with intimate (non-sexual) bonding

jokes.text: Die Hard!
jokes.setup: Joey alone after the awkward nap revelation
jokes.punchline: Still enthusiastically yelling about Die Hard by himself
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey's obliviousness to the drama around him, still focused on movies

jokes.text: Now we mince the shallots like this. Look, honey, the nice people are chopping shallots. Maybe if they chop enough of them, they can open that bikini shot.
jokes.setup: Monica watches what appears to be a cooking show
jokes.punchline: The cooking show is bizarrely connected to the topless carwash/bikini shop storyline
jokes.explanation: Surreal TV programming joke that's more weird than funny - points for originality but doesn't quite land

jokes.text: Not judging you, a little gay.
jokes.setup: Chandler suggests woman meant 'good luck taming that wild stallion'
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt assessment of his metaphor choice
jokes.explanation: Monica's characteristic directness puncturing Chandler's attempt at masculine bravado

jokes.text: Any chance you were looking into a bright, shiny thing called a mirror?
jokes.setup: Chandler describes the woman who wished Monica luck
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically suggests he was looking at his own reflection
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic comeback with good wordplay around the mirror metaphor

jokes.text: No, I only dated two girls in college... both blond, both not attractive.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if it was someone he dated in college
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-aware assessment of his limited and unsuccessful dating history
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor that fits Chandler's character and his history of dating struggles

jokes.text: No, we're still together.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if he broke up with his camp girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan suggestion they're still dating after 16+ years
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery - the absurdity of still dating a camp girlfriend decades later

jokes.text: I said fa...
jokes.setup: Chandler almost says 'fat' but stops himself
jokes.punchline: His inability to complete the word while still clearly communicating it
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment showing Chandler's discomfort with his past shallow behavior

jokes.text: You know what they say: 'Elephants never forget.'
jokes.setup: Chandler asks if Julie still feels bad about the breakup
jokes.punchline: Monica uses the elephant saying as a cruel fat joke about Julie's memory
jokes.explanation: Clever but dark wordplay - repurposing a common saying as a cruel fat joke, well-structured but morally questionable

jokes.text: Hope it goes better than the last time you did it for that girl downstairs.
jokes.setup: Phoebe is announced as maid of honor
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals Phoebe has failed at this role before
jokes.explanation: Good setup for revealing Phoebe's maid of honor history and Monica's awareness of potential problems

jokes.text: That's it. I am maid of honor. - No, I am. - How come you are? Because I cared enough to lie.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Phoebe argue over who should be maid of honor
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's logic that lying shows more care/investment
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe logic - turning dishonesty into a virtue with perfectly twisted reasoning only she could deliver

jokes.text: Then they send you a script. You go to the set. You rehearse and you have wardrobe fittings. Then you shoot your part. And it's great. But right after that, you're back out on the street looking for work again. Right back where you started. So I gotta say, I don't think a career in acting is the right choice for you two.
jokes.setup: Joey explains how acting auditions work as analogy for maid of honor selection
jokes.punchline: He gets completely sidetracked describing his career struggles and discourages them from acting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey misdirection - starts explaining auditions, gets lost in his own career anxiety, ends up giving unrelated advice

jokes.text: - Can't we just flip a coin? - No, coins hate me.
jokes.setup: Rachel suggests flipping a coin to decide
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's belief that inanimate objects have personal vendettas against her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe absurdity - her mystical worldview where coins can hold grudges, delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Wow, this cologne really is every bit as good as Giorgio.
jokes.setup: Phoebe tries to butter up Ross as a judge
jokes.punchline: Her over-the-top flattery about his cologne quality
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Phoebe's transparent attempt at flattery with oddly specific cologne comparison

jokes.text: That was the best nap I ever had.
jokes.setup: Joey wants to talk about the nap he and Ross shared
jokes.punchline: His earnest confession about the quality of their intimate nap
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Joey's innocent enthusiasm about something Ross finds deeply uncomfortable

jokes.text: - Sure. What do you got? - Warm milk and Excedrin PM.
jokes.setup: Ross asks what Joey has to drink
jokes.punchline: Joey only has sleep aids, revealing his nap obsession
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Joey's commitment to napping, both items specifically designed to induce sleep

jokes.text: - You're Louis Posen. - Who? He was my best friend in fifth grade. One day, I asked him to be my boyfriend and he said no.
jokes.setup: Monica figures out who Chandler reminds her of
jokes.punchline: Parallel between Chandler rejecting Julie and childhood rejection Monica experienced
jokes.explanation: Good character connection showing Monica's empathy through personal experience, sets up her investment in the apology

jokes.text: Because you kept talking to him while he was trying to go to the bathroom?
jokes.setup: Monica asks if Chandler knows why Louis rejected her
jokes.punchline: Chandler's assumption it was about Monica's oversharing tendencies
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals Chandler's awareness of Monica's boundary issues while avoiding the weight topic

jokes.text: I'll do it. But I warn you, this may make me a better person. And that is not the man you fell in love with.
jokes.setup: Chandler agrees to apologize to Julie
jokes.punchline: His warning that becoming better might ruin their relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor about personal growth potentially being relationship poison - very Chandler

jokes.text: - One being the highest. - Ten is the highest. - Why is 10 the highest? - Because it's the highest.
jokes.setup: Joey and Ross try to explain their scoring system
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that explains nothing while being confidently delivered
jokes.explanation: Good masculine incompetence humor - their inability to explain simple concepts clearly

jokes.text: And also, I ran out on a wedding. You don't get to keep the gifts.
jokes.setup: Rachel gives advice about wedding cold feet
jokes.punchline: Her practical addendum based on personal experience with wedding disasters
jokes.explanation: Good callback to series history with practical wisdom only Rachel could offer from experience

jokes.text: Ow! - Very good! - Oh. Yes, excellent. Perfect score.
jokes.setup: Phoebe tackles the 'drunk uncle' in the wedding scenario
jokes.punchline: She apparently gets physically violent and the judges praise her for it
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment showing Phoebe's direct approach to problem-solving and the judges' questionable standards

jokes.text: Hey, you want a little taste of Pheebs?
jokes.setup: Rachel complains about Phoebe making a scene
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's threatening offer to demonstrate her violent capabilities
jokes.explanation: Good intimidating Phoebe moment - her casual threat delivered with characteristic directness

jokes.text: This situation is not sanctioned!
jokes.setup: Ross tries to maintain order when Joey improvises a scenario
jokes.punchline: His overly serious/official response to Joey's joke scenario
jokes.explanation: Good Ross control-freak moment - treating a silly game with bureaucratic seriousness

jokes.text: Webster's Dictionary defines marriage as... Okay, no! Forget that! That sucks! Okay, never mind. Forget it.
jokes.setup: Rachel starts her maid of honor speech
jokes.punchline: She immediately recognizes and abandons the cliché opening
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about terrible speech openings with realistic self-correction

jokes.text: ...although he seemed like a 6-year-old.
jokes.setup: Rachel describes meeting Monica at 6 and Chandler at 25
jokes.punchline: Her assessment that adult Chandler had the maturity of a child
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based humor that fits both Rachel's perspective and Chandler's established immaturity

jokes.text: I remember talking about this day with Rachel... while we were showering together, naked. And she's back in the game!
jokes.setup: Phoebe starts a sweet speech about anticipating Monica's wedding
jokes.punchline: Completely derails it with inappropriate sexual reference, Joey's excited reaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - takes what seems like a heartfelt moment and destroys it with calculated inappropriateness

jokes.text: Hello, Skidmark.
jokes.setup: Chandler introduces himself to Julie, expecting recognition
jokes.punchline: Julie remembers him by an embarrassing nickname
jokes.explanation: Great setup payoff - reveals Chandler had additional humiliating camp baggage beyond just breaking up with her

jokes.text: It's a nickname. I'll explain later. It's pretty clear.
jokes.setup: Chandler offers to explain the nickname 'Skidmark'
jokes.punchline: Monica's immediate understanding makes explanation unnecessary and more embarrassing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation - the nickname is self-explanatory in the worst way possible

jokes.text: You didn't know that?
jokes.setup: Chandler apologizes for breaking up with Julie due to weight gain
jokes.punchline: Discovers she was unaware of the real reason, making his apology worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe escalation - Chandler's attempt to make amends actually creates new damage

jokes.text: Well, I guess my work here is done.
jokes.setup: Chandler realizes he's made things much worse
jokes.punchline: His deadpan conclusion that he's accomplished his mission despite causing more harm
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan capper to the cringe sequence - Chandler's self-aware acknowledgment of his failure

jokes.text: Well, except Rachel. Damn it!
jokes.setup: Ross says there are no losers in the competition
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by naming Rachel as the loser, her reaction
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with immediate contradiction and Rachel's frustrated response

jokes.text: - Actually, it wasn't that close.
jokes.setup: Ross says the competition was really close
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt honesty that it wasn't close at all
jokes.explanation: Good Joey honesty moment - his inability to maintain polite fiction when delivering harsh truth

jokes.text: What? The coins have finally forgiven me!
jokes.setup: Phoebe wins the coin flip
jokes.punchline: Her belief that inanimate objects have ended their grudge against her
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier absurdity - Phoebe's mystical relationship with currency resolved

jokes.text: vegetarian-voodoo-goddess-circle-y shower
jokes.setup: Rachel predicts what kind of shower Phoebe will throw
jokes.punchline: String of adjectives capturing Phoebe's alternative lifestyle approach to events
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor capturing Rachel's perception of Phoebe's unconventional approach to traditional events

jokes.text: A hundred and forty-five pounds. In one year? My God, what did she eat? Her family?
jokes.setup: Chandler reveals how much weight Julie gained
jokes.punchline: Monica's shock and dark humor about cannibalistic eating
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation that's shocking but fits the show's occasionally mean-spirited comedy about weight

jokes.text: You were not supposed to hear that. I said that behind your back.
jokes.setup: Monica points out Chandler called her fat at Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defense that insulting her behind her back makes it different/better
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler logic - his genuine belief that the method of insult delivery matters more than the insult itself

jokes.text: This is one of the things that I can apologize for later.
jokes.setup: Chandler realizes mid-sentence he's saying something wrong
jokes.punchline: His meta-commentary on his own communication failures
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware humor showing Chandler's recognition of his pattern while being unable to stop it

jokes.text: I'd carry you around in my pocket.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if he'd love her even if she shrank to 2 inches tall
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sweet but absurd image of pocket-sized Monica
jokes.explanation: Sweet romantic comedy - absurd but genuinely touching response that maintains humor while being romantic

jokes.text: That's ridiculous. Rachel, we were all babies once. Oh, you mean today. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Rachel apologizes for being 'a baby'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe takes it literally before realizing the metaphorical meaning
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe literal interpretation humor with perfect timing on the correction

jokes.text: She made me carry her train... which was weird because I was Wonder Woman.
jokes.setup: Rachel describes Halloween when Monica dressed as a bride
jokes.punchline: The visual absurdity of Wonder Woman carrying a bride's train
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - the incongruity of superhero costume hierarchy being subverted

jokes.text: No, I was 10. I just developed early. Man alive!
jokes.setup: Phoebe says Rachel's been thinking about this since age 14
jokes.punchline: Rachel corrects to 10, Phoebe's shocked reaction to the implication
jokes.explanation: Decent character humor about Rachel's early obsession with weddings and Phoebe's reaction

jokes.text: Yeah, okay. You laugh now. She's gonna be yours.
jokes.setup: Monica excitedly talks about intensive wedding planning meetings
jokes.punchline: Rachel's warning to Phoebe about what she's getting into
jokes.explanation: Good setup for future problems - Rachel's experience-based warning about Monica's intensity

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing? God!
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey are discovered in their napping position
jokes.punchline: Chandler's shocked reaction to finding them cuddling
jokes.explanation: Solid ending physical comedy - Chandler walking in on the culmination of the nap subplot

tmdb_overview: Joey and Ross accidentally take a nap together - and much to their dismay, find that they liked it. Phoebe and Rachel compete to be Monica's maid of honor.
